title: Car of CPM whistleblower V Kunhikrishnan’s backroom strategist torched, house vandalised in Payyannur
summary: The house of T Purushothaman, a Marxist intellectual, at Mahadeva Gramam in Payyannur, was vandalised in the early hours of Friday.

title: Kerala HC pulls up Devaswom Board over accounting lapses, orders centralised system
summary: Recording the findings, the court noted that the issues were not isolated irregularities but systemic in nature.

title: Key events in Kerala today: Vishu Handloom Fair, painting exhibition mark April 10
summary: Kerala events today feature a diverse range of activities, including cultural activities, educational conferences, and fairs.

title: After polls, LDF ready to concede Taliparamba; BJP hopes fade amid high turnout in Manjeshwar, Kasaragod
summary: The recent election saw a notable surge in voter turnout, particularly among first-time voters in Kasaragod, driven by a Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls

title: '₹15,000 for 1 vote': Sivankutty alleges 'cash-for-vote' by BJP in Nemom
summary: V Sivankutty, the LDF candidate in Nemom and General Education Minister, accused the BJP of engaging in unethical practices to influence voters.

title: Analysis | Spike in voter turnout signals wave or hung verdict?
summary: Even then, compared to 2021, an additional 9.21 lakh voted this time.

title: Analysis | Central Kerala shows surge in turnout, but is there a wave?
summary: In Thripunithura, heavy polling day turnout may still fall short of the 2021 total votes.

title: Analysis | High turnout and high hopes for fronts in ‘king-maker’ Malabar
summary: With Kozhikode district posting the highest turnout in the state (81%), LDF, UDF and the NDA have claimed that the trend is in their favour.

title: ‘What upset me most about politics…’ Pisharody opens up as a first-time candidate
summary: In a heated battle, Pisharody faced allegations of making ‘anti-women’ remarks and his aim for entering politics was questioned by rivals.

title: A party booth in Ernakulam where Congress, CPM and BJP share one roof, one history
summary: Just yesterday, BJP workers struggled to find a booth and had resigned to camping out in the open, under the sun.
